questions: Should resolve-library-item.ts support all three resolution strategies (cuid, ticket short ID, title)?
How should the library module handle the nested 'comments' subcommand routing?
Should 'hlx library show' render full markdown content or just headings with metadata?
Does the hxFetch basePath need to change for library commands?

answers.answer: Yes, all three strategies. Resolution priority: (1) cuid — if input starts with 'c' and is 25 chars, treat as library item ID. (2) ticket short ID — if input matches XXX-NNN pattern, resolve the ticket, then find the latest library item for that ticket via GET /api/library/items?ticketShortId=X. (3) title substring — fallback to case-insensitive title match across GET /api/library/items list. This parallels resolve-ticket.ts (exact ID > short ID > numeric) and provides intuitive CLI ergonomics.
evidence: src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts: extractTicketRef and matchTicket support ID, shortId, and number with priority ordering
Research report: 'Three resolution strategies following resolve-ticket.ts'

answers.answer: Nested switch-case in a comments.ts router, following the same pattern as the top-level index.ts dispatcher. The library module's index.ts dispatches 'comments' to runLibraryComments(config, args), which itself dispatches 'list' to cmdCommentsListLibrary and 'post' to cmdCommentsPostLibrary. This mirrors how the existing comments module (src/comments/index.ts) dispatches to cmdCommentsList and cmdCommentsPost.
evidence: src/comments/index.ts: runComments dispatches to cmdCommentsList and cmdCommentsPost based on subcommand
src/index.ts: Top-level switch dispatches to runX(config, args.slice(1))

answers.answer: Render headings with section slugs and comment summaries, plus a truncated content preview per section. Full markdown content in a terminal is unwieldy (reports can be 1000+ lines). The show command extracts heading lines from the markdown content, annotates each with its slug and comment summary counts, and shows the first ~3 lines of content under each heading. A --full flag can output the complete content. This is the most useful format for agents and users deciding which sections to comment on.
evidence: Research report: 'Shows report content with section headings annotated with slugs and comment summaries'
The primary purpose of 'show' is to discover section slugs for the --section flag in comments post

answers.answer: Library commands use basePath: '/api' (not the default '/api/inspect'). This is the same basePath used by the existing comments module for non-inspect endpoints. The hxFetch utility supports this via the options.basePath parameter.
evidence: src/lib/http.ts: hxFetch accepts basePath option, defaults to '/api/inspect'
src/comments/list.ts and post.ts use basePath: '/api' for ticket comment endpoints

statement: The CLI needs a new src/library/ module following the established switch-case routing pattern with a nested comments subcommand router. resolve-library-item.ts supports cuid, ticket short ID, and title match resolution. 'hlx library show' renders heading-level summaries with slugs and comment counts (not full content by default). All commands use basePath '/api' with the existing hxFetch client. SKILL.md update is critical for agent discoverability.
